name: Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop
name_original: Embalse del Cenajo — circular desde Las Minas y Yesares de Hellín
description: Mountain biking the Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop offers a moderate 26.9-mile ride with varied terrain and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Hellín offer diverse terrain, from river valleys to unique geological formations. The region, situated in the province of Albacete, features a mix of natural landscapes suitable for various skill levels. Riders can explore paths along the Mundo and Segura Rivers, through areas with distinctive rock formations, and around natural protected lagoons. The elevation changes provide varied challenges, from moderate climbs to more demanding ascents. Best mountain bike trails around Hellín The most popular mountain bike trail is Desde Hellín a Tobarra — circular en BTT, a 19.3 miles (31.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects Hellín with the nearby town of Tobarra. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop, a moderate 26.9 miles (43.3 km) path. This route offers scenic views along the Segura River and around the Cenajo Reservoir. Local mountain bikers also love the Hellín, Cancarix Volcano and Cenajo Dam MTB Loop, a 57.2 miles (92.0 km) trail leading through the unique landscape surrounding the Cancarix Volcano, often completed in about 8 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Hellín is defined by river valleys, volcanic landscapes, and protected lagoons. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to difficult, longer routes. The routes in Hellín are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Hellín's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Hellín?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Hellín, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 14 easy routes, 45 moderate routes, and 6 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Hellín?
A: Hellín's mountain bike trails feature diverse terrain, from river valleys along the Mundo and Segura Rivers to unique geological formations like the Cancarix Volcano. You'll also find paths around natural protected lagoons and areas with distinctive rock formations, providing varied challenges and scenic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Hellín?
A: Yes, Hellín offers several family-friendly options. There are 14 easy routes suitable for beginners or families looking for a relaxed ride. The peri-urban path designed by the Hellín City Council, which connects to 'El Arboreto de Isso' and 'La Laguna de los Patos', is a good example of a route that combines natural beauty with educational stops, making it suitable for families.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see while mountain biking in Hellín?
A: Hellín's trails pass by several unique natural features. You can explore the area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and Quípar River Canyon&lt;/a&gt;, or ride near the ancient Cancarix Volcano. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín – Laguna de los Patos loop from Hellin&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the Laguna de los Patos, a natural protected area known for birdwatching. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746731" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Confluence of the Moratalla River and the Segura River&lt;/a&gt; also offers picturesque scenery.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1223181593/hellin-cancarix-volcano-and-cenajo-dam-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hellín, Cancarix Volcano and Cenajo Dam MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 92.0 km trail that takes over 8 hours to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobarra Village – Tobarra loop from Hellin&lt;/a&gt;, a 67.5 km difficult route.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Hellín?
A: The mountain bike trails in Hellín are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from river valleys to unique geological formations, and the well-maintained paths that cater to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Hellín?
A: Yes, many routes in Hellín are circular, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same location. Examples include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1223169991/desde-hellin-a-tobarra-circular-en-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desde Hellín a Tobarra — circular en BTT&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187080889/rio-segura-and-cenajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find trails that offer scenic viewpoints in the Hellín area?
A: Yes, the diverse landscape around Hellín provides several opportunities for scenic viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Serreta Cave&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight, and routes passing by the Alfonso XIII Reservoir Dam and the Confluence of the Mundo and Segura Rivers offer impressive vistas of the natural surroundings.

Q: What is the best season for mountain biking in Hellín?
A: Hellín's climate generally allows for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for riding. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable, while winters are usually mild, though some days might be cooler.

Q: Are there any archaeological sites or historical points of interest accessible by mountain bike near Hellín?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. The El Tolmo de Minateda Archaeological Park, with its UNESCO heritage sites and ancient cave paintings, is a significant cultural landmark that can be combined with mountain biking adventures in the area. While not directly on a komoot MTB route, its proximity makes it an interesting point of exploration.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features like rivers or reservoirs?
A: Many trails in Hellín incorporate water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1187080889/rio-segura-and-cenajo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers scenic views along the Segura River and around the Cenajo Reservoir. You can also find routes that explore the picturesque confluence of the Mundo and Segura Rivers, often referred to as the 'end of the world'.
tips: The dam is not only a masterpiece of engineering, but also an impressive viewpoint.
A great place to rest for a moment and admire the water and natural beauty. Both the reservoir and the Camarillas dam are located in the Almadenes Canyon through which the Mundo River runs.
Abandoned sulfur mines that give their name to the nearby nucleus (Las Minas), are located in the Yesares de Hellín Microreserve.

faqs.question: How difficult is the mountain biking on the Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: This route is officially rated as moderate. While some sections feature paved surfaces suitable for most riders, be prepared for challenging unpaved segments with loose gravel and hard climbing. Some parts may even require pushing your bike. Good to very good fitness is recommended for the more demanding sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied. You'll encounter sections with smooth paved surfaces, but also rugged unpaved trails, tight singletrack, and areas with loose gravel. The broader Sierra de Segura, which this route traverses, features mountainous terrain, gray, rocky features, rooted areas, and some flowy sections. It's a diverse ride!

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop?
faqs.answer: This route offers several notable sights. You'll pass by the impressive Cenajo Dam and the Camarillas Dam. Additionally, you can explore the historical Old sulfur mines, adding a cultural dimension to the natural beauty.

faqs.question: Is the Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop a circular route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the name suggests, this is a loop trail, meaning you'll end up back near your starting point without having to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail, and is there public transport available?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, routes in this region often start from villages or designated recreational spots. It's advisable to check local maps or komoot's starting point for the most convenient parking. Public transport options directly to the trailhead may be limited, so driving is often the most practical way to access the area.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The region experiences diverse weather. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on this route, especially within the Microrreserva Yesares de Hellín?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Microrreserva Yesares de Hellín. Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for mountain biking on public trails in this area. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be aware of any signage regarding protected areas.

faqs.question: Is the Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, specific regulations for this particular route or within the Microrreserva Yesares de Hellín may vary. It's best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in natural reserves, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all trail users.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while mountain biking this route?
faqs.answer: The scenery is a major highlight. You'll experience the striking turquoise waters of the Río Segura and Cenajo Reservoir set against barren hillsides. The surrounding Sierra de Segura also features pine forests and almond plantations. Keep an eye out for glimpses of medieval villages and historical structures that add cultural richness to the natural beauty.

faqs.question: Does this mountain biking route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 32% of this route follows sections of the official Río Segura trail, offering a connected experience with the wider network of paths in the region.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is primarily natural, the region has charming medieval villages. It's likely you'll find cafes or restaurants in nearby towns, especially those popular with visitors, where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's always a good idea to carry enough water and snacks for the duration of your trip.
